name: Quebec Apple Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F. Make enough pie dough to roll into 12 6" x 6" squares (about a double batch). Sprinkle each square with 1 teaspoon grated cheddar.
Peel and core apples and place one on each square. Fill the center of each apple with 2 teaspoons (or 1 heaping teaspoon) of minced meat, 1 teaspoon rum, a dot of butter, and 1 tablespoon sugar. Bring diagonally opposite corners of dough over the apple to form ears at the top. Cover the 'ears' with aluminum foil.
Mix the brown sugar, cream, and grated lemon rind. Brush the dumplings with this mixture and bake for 35 to 45 minutes in a 400°F oven.
Remove foil, brush the ears with cream mixture, and return to oven until 'ears' are brown. Serve warm topped with plain cream or sweetened cream, flavored with rum.
Note: The original recipe does not specify total amounts for cheese, sugar, minced meat, and rum. The author used Meal Master's conversion to decimal and back to calculate the total amounts.
Source: The Canadiana Cookbook by Mme. Jehane Benoit
